# checks.typing.TypingCheck { #pycmdcheck.checks.typing.TypingCheck }

```python
checks.typing.TypingCheck()
```

Run type checking tools on the package.

Executes a configurable static type checker to verify type
annotations. Supports mypy (default) and pyright.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The check identifier ("typing").

<code>[**description**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Human-readable description of this check.

<code>[**SUPPORTED_TOOLS**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   List of supported type checking tools.

## Configuration Options {.doc-section .doc-section-configuration-options}

tool (str): Type checker to use ("mypy" or "pyright").
    Defaults to "mypy".
strict (bool): Whether to enable strict mode. Defaults to False.
args (list[str]): Additional arguments to pass to the type checker.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Run type checking with default settings (mypy):

```python
>>> from pathlib import Path
>>> check = TypingCheck()
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), {"enabled": True})
>>> result.name
'typing'
```

Configure to use pyright:

```python
>>> config = {"enabled": True, "tool": "pyright"}
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), config)
```

Enable strict mode:

```python
>>> config = {"enabled": True, "strict": True}
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), config)
```

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [run](#pycmdcheck.checks.typing.TypingCheck.run) | Run type checking on the package. |

### run { #pycmdcheck.checks.typing.TypingCheck.run }

```python
checks.typing.TypingCheck.run(package_path, config)
```

Run type checking on the package.

Executes the configured type checker and collects any errors found.
Errors are reported in the result details (limited to first 10).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**package_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Path](`pathlib.Path`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to the package directory to check.

<code>[**config**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Configuration dictionary with options:  - tool (str): "mypy" or "pyright" (default: "mypy") - strict (bool): Enable strict mode (default: False) - args (list[str]): Additional arguments for the checker

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A CheckResult with:

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - OK status if no type errors found

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - ERROR status if type errors found or checker fails

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - SKIPPED status if the type checker is not installed
